content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 10, 1977

Mr. Edward F. Buckner
County Counsel
County of Sutter
463 Second Street
Yuba City, California 95591

Dear Mr. Buckner:

This is in response to your letter to Ms. Wiser of our staff dated
20 April 1977.

You ask whether persons holding acting appointments to an office
specified in Government Code Section 87200 are under an obligation to file
a statement of economic interest, and if so, what time constraints are
involved in such filing. With respect to the first matter, it is the
staff's opinion that persons holding such an office for a significant
period of time and under circumstances in which the person previously
holding the office has no intention of returning are obliged to file state-
ments pursuant to Government Code Sections 87202 and 87203. As I understand
the circumstances of your situation, the appointment to the position
of County Administrative Officer is for a period of at least eight months
and the person previously holding the office will not be returning.
These circumstances are clearly distinguishable from a situation where an
officeholder described in Section 87200 is on a brief and finite leave of
absence for personal health or other reasons.

With respect to your second question, Government Code Section 87202
provides, in pertinent part, that:

"...Every person who is appointed to an office specified
in Section 87200 shall file such a statement not less than
ten days prior to assuming office."

We recognize that assumption of such an office may in some cases be
dictated by emergency circumstances. Government Code Section 87302(b), in
discussing the remedial provisions of conflict of interest codes, specifically
recognizes this by providing that, "...All other new designated employees
shall file statements not less than ten days before assuming office or,
if subject to confirmation, ten days before being confirmed, unless an
earlier assumption of office is required by emergency circumstances. (emphasis
supplied). However, no similar provision is found in Section 87202.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

Mr. Edward F. Buckner
May 10, 1977
page two

Accordingly, we are not of the opinion that emergency circumstances would
create a waiver of the obligations imposed by Section 87202.

However, as Ms. Wiser indicated in her letter of April 15, the
Commission may, and frequently has, waived fines for late filing imposed
pursuant to Government Code Section 91013 where there is a "justifiable
excuse" for such late filing. The type of emergency circumstances described
in your letter would, in all probability, constitute a "justifiable excuse."
Filing of the statement should still be accomplished as quickly as practicable.
Accordingly, I would reiterate Ms. Wiser's advice to please inform the
Commission of the reasons for the late filing in order that we may make a
determination on this matter.

If I can be of any further assistance, please feel free to call me at
(916) 322-6444.

Sincerely,

Kenneth W. Goshorn
Research Specialist III
Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa

EDWARD F. BUCKNER
County Counsel

CLAUDE L. BIDDLE, JR.
GARBE [illegible] RHOMB
Deputy County Counsel

DARRELL W. LARSEN
Deputy County Counsel

OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SUTTER
463 SECOND STREET
YUBA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95991

PHONE [illegible] 674-8650

April 20, 1977

Dawn Wiser
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Wiser:

     I am in receipt of your letter of April 15, copy attached;
also attached is a copy of the minutes of the Sutter County
Board of Supervisors.

     I am currently, and have been for the past nine years,
County Counsel of this county; and on January 11, 1977, I was
also appointed Acting County Administrative Officer so that I
am not in effect holding two positions.

     The addition of the duties of the County Administrative
Officer offers no additional salary; however, it does offer
great rewards in that it allows me to work many extra hours
without compensation, together with the assumption of many
problems not associated with my duties as County Counsel.

     In the past, we have had acting appointments for various
officers but I find no clear authority that requires disclosure
by a person appointed in an acting role:  However, I have ad-
vised, as County Counsel, that a disclosure be filed as a
courtesy if for no other reason.

     Acting appointments are made for many reasons and for
various terms; they could be for one day or for longer unspecified periods.

     Many acting appointments are made because of emergency
situations, such as death of the incumbent.  The acting appointment is made immediately after the death.  It would appear that
an acting appointment would always be in violation since death

Ms. Dawn Wiser
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page two
April 20, 1977


cannot ordinarily be anticipated precisely ten days in advance.

     Please advise of the legal authority requiring disclosure by
an acting officer, and at what point the acting officer should
assume office in an emergency situation so as to avoid the ten
day filing before assuming office. Your opinion in this regard
will assist me as County Counsel in advising my clients in
similar situations.

     Thank you for your courtesy.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        EDWARD F. BUCKNER
                                        COUNTY COUNSEL


EFB:pb
Attachments

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 15, 1977


Honorable Edward F. Buckner
Sutter County Administrative Officer
1225 Maple Ave.
Yuba City, CA  95591

Dear Mr. Buckner:

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has received
your Statement of Economic Interests executed on January 31,
1977. The Political Reform Act provides that every person
who is appointed to the office you hold shall file a Statement
of Economic Interests not less than ten (10) days prior to
assuming office (Government Code Sections 87200, 87202). It
further provides that if a Statement is filed after its
deadline, the late filer is liable in the amount of $10 per
day, up to a maximum of $100 (Government Code Section 91013).
Since you filed your Statement 30 days late, you are subject
to a fine of $100.

     If there is a justifiable excuse for your filing
late, please inform the Commission of that excuse in writing
within twenty (20) days so that a waiver of your fine can be
considered. If no such excuse exists, please remit to this
office within twenty (20) days a check or money order in the
amount of $100, made payable to the "State of California."
The full amount will be deposited into the General Fund of
the State of California.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Dawn Wiser
                                        Dawn Wiser

cc:  County Clerk


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION: (916) 322-5660 · EXECUTIVE/LEGAL: (222-5201) · ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST: 322-6441

REPORT ON TENTATIVE MAPS

Mr. Larson reported on Planning Commission's action on tentative
map for H.D. & Bertha Richter, et al.

******

Mr. Larson from Planning asked the Board members if they are
going to request that a presentation be made relative to the new EIR
Guidelines next week.     It was the consensus of the Board that a
study session be scheduled relative to these new guidelines. Mr.
Larson to work with County Counsel Buckner on setting up a study
session relative to discussing the new guidelines of the EIR.

******

The Board adjourned into an executive session relative to
personnel.

******

APPOINTMENT TO
COUNTY ADMINISTRATOR POSITION

On motion of Supervisor Taylor, seconded by Supervisor Green
and unanimously carried, the Board hereby appoints E.F. Buckner, as
Acting County Administrator for approximately 6 to 8 months on a trial
basis to see how this will work out with the option to change their
decision at any time.

******

STUDY SESSION
TAX PROTEST

Dr. Libby, Chairman of the Taxpayer's Association, appeared
and requested to have this session recorded, the Board gave their
okay.

Mr. Hal Simpson appeared and stated that the theme the Taxpayers
would like to have today is everyone to work together for a benefit
to Sutter County. Mr. Simpson gave the reasons that the taxpayers
have listed on their tax forms as to why they were filing under protest.
Mr. Simpson stated that there are 1379 taxpayers paying taxes under
protest this year and he feels that the main reason they filed under
protest is that they have no idea what this money is being used for
and would like to find out where it all goes MINUTE BOOK 1-S PAGE 350
JANUARY 11, 1977

sections.question: You ask whether persons holding acting appointments to an office specified in Government Code Section 87200 are under an obligation to file a statement of economic interest, and if so, what are constraints are involved in such filing.
sections.conclusion: It is the staff's Opinion that persons holding such an office for a significant period of time and under circumstances in which the person previously holding the office has no intention of returning are obliged to file statements of interests pursuant to Government Code Sections 87202 and 87203. While emergency circumstances would not create a waiver of obligations imposed by Section 87202, the Commission may waive late filing fees where there is a 'justifiable excuse,' and the emergency circumstances described would likely constitute such an excuse. Filing should be accomplished as quickly as practicable.
sections.question_synthetic: Must persons holding acting appointments to offices under Government Code Section 87200 file statements of economic interest, and what constraints apply to such filing, particularly in emergency situations?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff concludes that persons in acting positions for significant periods intend to stay are required to file economic interest statements, and while emergency circumstances cannot waive this requirement, they may constitute justifiable excuse for late filing under Government Code Section 91013. The filing deadline cannot be waived by emergency circumstances as it can for other designated positions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains the incoming request letter from County Counsel Buckner dated April 20, 1977, initial FPPC notice dated April 15, 1977, and the final FPPC response dated May 10, 1977. Some OCR errors present in original document (e.g., 'covennent' for 'Government', 'Circwrsmnms' for 'Circumstances'). Board minutes attached show the appointment was made January 11, 1977 for approximately 5-24 months on trial basis.

QUESTION: You ask whether persons holding acting appointments to an office specified in Government Code Section 87200 are under an obligation to file a statement of economic interest, and if so, what are constraints are involved in such filing.

CONCLUSION: It is the staff's Opinion that persons holding such an office for a significant period of time and under circumstances in which the person previously holding the office has no intention of returning are obliged to file statements of interests pursuant to Government Code Sections 87202 and 87203. While emergency circumstances would not create a waiver of obligations imposed by Section 87202, the Commission may waive late filing fees where there is a 'justifiable excuse,' and the emergency circumstances described would likely constitute such an excuse. Filing should be accomplished as quickly as practicable.

embedding.summary: This advice letter responds to a county counsel's inquiry regarding whether acting officers must file statements of economic interest under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC advises that acting appointees holding positions for significant periods with no expectation of the previous officeholder returning must file statements, and while emergency circumstances do not waive the requirement, they may justify late filing fees.
